content.text: Rollback Drill Exercises and Verification

A rollback runbook that has never been executed is a hypothesis, not a plan. The only way to know whether a rollback will work under pressure is to run it in advance, measure the time taken, and identify the gaps between the documented procedure and what actually happens. Rollback drills are structured exercises that answer a single question: can this team return the service to a known-good state within the defined SLA, following only the runbook?

For GenAI services, drills have an additional function: they validate the metric collection pipeline used to detect rollback triggers. A drill that goes wrong because the Prometheus query returned stale data or the feature flag CLI was not installed on the on-call engineer's machine is a drill that found a gap before it cost users.

attrs.alt: Drill program table: Feature Flag Drill (monthly, 60s target), Full Service Rollback (quarterly, 7min target), Data Migration Rollback (before each migration), Game Day (semi-annual, surprise failure injection). Timeline shows 5-step feature flag drill sequence from T+0 (alert fires) to T+60s (status posted, PASS).

content.text: Drill Types and Cadence
Four drill types cover the full rollback surface for a GenAI service. Each targets a different failure mode and requires different participants.
Drill Type
Frequency
Duration
What Is Tested
Feature Flag Drill
Monthly
15 min
Speed of flag disable, canary traffic drain, on-call tool familiarity
Full Service Rollback Drill
Quarterly
45 min
Image rollback via kubectl, pod readiness, health check pass
Data Migration Rollback Drill
Before each migration
60 min
Rollback path for the specific migration about to ship
Game Day (Chaos Exercise)
Semi-annual
Half day
Detection, triage, rollback, and communication under surprise conditions

Drills should always run in staging, never in production. The staging environment must be close enough to production (same deployment tooling, same flag keys, same Prometheus targets) that drill results are meaningful.
Drill 1: Feature Flag Rollback

The feature flag drill tests the fastest rollback path: disabling the canary routing flag and confirming that canary traffic drops to zero within 60 seconds. This is the most frequently executed drill because it is the lowest-cost and covers the most common rollback scenario.
Procedure
On-call engineer is notified of the drill via PagerDuty (same as a real incident -- do not tell them it is a drill until afterward).
Engineer opens the rollback runbook URL from the PagerDuty alert.
Engineer executes the feature flag disable command from the runbook.
Engineer confirms canary traffic is zero using the Prometheus query from the runbook.
Engineer posts a status update to the incident channel.
Drill facilitator records the elapsed time at each step.
Flag is re-enabled and staging is restored.
Automated Drill Runner

# scripts/run_rollback_drill.py
import time, subprocess, sys, json

FLAG_KEY = 'genai-assistant-v2'
PROM = 'https://prometheus-staging.internal/api/v1/query'

def query_prom(q: str) -> float:
    import urllib.request, urllib.parse
    url = f'{PROM}?query={urllib.parse.quote(q)}'
    with urllib.request.urlopen(url) as r:
        data = json.load(r)
    result = data['data']['result']
    return float(result[0]['value'][1]) if result else 0.0

def run_flag_drill() -> dict:
    results = {}
    t0 = time.time()

    # Step 1: Disable the canary flag
    subprocess.check_call([
        'launchdarkly-cli', 'flag', 'set',
        '--flag', FLAG_KEY, '--value', 'false',
        '--comment', 'rollback-drill',
    ])
    results['flag_disabled_at'] = time.time() - t0

    # Step 2: Wait for traffic to drain
    for _ in range(10):
        time.sleep(6)
        rps = query_prom(f"rate(genai_requests_total{{slot='canary'}}[30s])")
        if rps < 0.01:
            break
    results['traffic_zero_at'] = time.time() - t0

    # Step 3: Re-enable to restore drill environment
    subprocess.check_call([
        'launchdarkly-cli', 'flag', 'set',
        '--flag', FLAG_KEY, '--value', 'true',
        '--comment', 'rollback-drill-restore',
    ])
    results['flag_restored_at'] = time.time() - t0
    results['pass'] = results['traffic_zero_at'] <= 60.0
    return results

if __name__ == '__main__':
    print('Running feature flag rollback drill...')
    r = run_flag_drill()
    print(json.dumps(r, indent=2))
    if not r['pass']:
        print('DRILL FAILED: traffic did not drop to zero within 60s')
        sys.exit(1)
    print('DRILL PASSED')
Pass Criteria
Metric
Target
Fail Condition
Time to flag disabled
< 30 seconds
Engineer could not find or execute the command
Time to canary traffic = 0
< 60 seconds
Flag cache delay exceeded, or query was wrong
Time to status posted
< 90 seconds
Communication template not found
Runbook gaps identified
0 critical
Any step required knowledge not in the runbook
Drill 2: Full Service Rollback
The full service rollback drill exercises the kubectl image rollback path. Unlike the flag drill, this requires a full rolling deployment to complete and tests the pod readiness probe timing.
Procedure
Facilitator deploys a 'bad' image version to the canary deployment in staging (an image that returns a deliberate error rate).
On-call engineer is paged with a simulated eval degradation alert.
Engineer executes the kubectl rollback commands from the runbook.
Engineer monitors the rollout with kubectl rollout status.
Engineer confirms the stable image is running and the error rate has returned to baseline.
Facilitator verifies the image tag in all pods matches the stable version.
Verification Script

#!/bin/bash
# scripts/verify_drill_outcomes.sh
# Run after a drill to confirm the staging environment is fully restored
set -e

NAMESPACE=staging
FLAG_KEY=genai-assistant-v2

echo '=== Post-Drill Verification ==='

# 1. Confirm feature flag is back to its pre-drill state
FLAG_VALUE=$(launchdarkly-cli flag get --flag $FLAG_KEY | grep '"value"' | tr -d ' ')
echo "Flag state: $FLAG_VALUE"

# 2. Confirm canary deployment is running expected replica count
READY=$(kubectl get deployment genai-api-canary -n $NAMESPACE \
  -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}')
DESIRED=$(kubectl get deployment genai-api-canary -n $NAMESPACE \
  -o jsonpath='{.spec.replicas}')
echo "Canary replicas: $READY / $DESIRED"

# 3. Confirm no artificial env vars are set
kubectl get deployment genai-api-canary -n $NAMESPACE \
  -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].env}' \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; envs=json.load(sys.stdin); \
    [print(f'  {e[\"name\"]}: {e[\"value\"]}') \
    for e in envs if any(k in e['name'] \
    for k in ['FAIL_RATE','DELAY','ERROR_RATE'])]"

# 4. Run a quick smoke test
curl -sf https://api-staging.internal/healthz | python3 -m json.tool | grep status

echo '=== Verification complete ==='
Drill 3: Game Day (Chaos Exercise)

A game day injects an unknown failure into the staging environment without telling the team which failure scenario was chosen. The goal is to test the full response pipeline -- alert firing, on-call paging, triage, runbook selection, rollback execution, and communication -- under realistic conditions where the root cause must be determined before the fix can be applied.
Game Day Design
Three or more failure scenarios are prepared in advance by the facilitator but kept secret from participants.
Scenarios should cover different root causes: eval degradation, latency regression, safety spike, cost overrun.
Each scenario is injected via an environment variable or feature flag change that can be restored instantly if the drill must be aborted.
Participants use only the runbooks, Slack, and Prometheus -- no direct facilitator help unless safety is at risk.
The facilitator records time-to-detection, time-to-triage, time-to-rollback, and time-to-all-clear.

# scripts/game_day_runner.py
# Injects failures into staging environment and records recovery time
import time, subprocess, json, random

FAILURE_SCENARIOS = [
    {'name': 'eval_collapse',
     'inject': 'kubectl set env deployment/genai-api-canary EVAL_FAIL_RATE=0.20 -n staging',
     'restore': 'kubectl set env deployment/genai-api-canary EVAL_FAIL_RATE=0 -n staging'},
    {'name': 'latency_spike',
     'inject': 'kubectl set env deployment/genai-api-canary ARTIFICIAL_DELAY_MS=1200 -n staging',
     'restore': 'kubectl set env deployment/genai-api-canary ARTIFICIAL_DELAY_MS=0 -n staging'},
    {'name': 'error_rate_spike',
     'inject': 'kubectl set env deployment/genai-api-canary HTTP_ERROR_RATE=0.03 -n staging',
     'restore': 'kubectl set env deployment/genai-api-canary HTTP_ERROR_RATE=0 -n staging'},
]

def run_game_day(scenario_name: str | None = None) -> dict:
    scenario = (
        next(s for s in FAILURE_SCENARIOS if s['name'] == scenario_name)
        if scenario_name
        else random.choice(FAILURE_SCENARIOS)
    )
    print(f"Injecting: {scenario['name']}")
    t0 = time.time()

    subprocess.check_call(scenario['inject'], shell=True)
    injection_time = time.time() - t0

    # Wait for team to detect and respond without being told the scenario
    print('Waiting for team response (check Slack for rollback execution)...')
    input('Press Enter once team has completed rollback: ')
    recovery_time = time.time() - t0

    subprocess.check_call(scenario['restore'], shell=True)

    return {
        'scenario': scenario['name'],
        'injection_time_s': injection_time,
        'recovery_time_s': recovery_time,
        'within_sla': recovery_time <= 420,  # 7-minute SLA
    }
Drill Scoring and Scorecards

Every drill produces a scorecard that records the actual elapsed time for each step against the target SLA. Scorecards are stored in version control alongside the runbooks, providing a historical record of drill performance and highlighting which steps are consistently slow.

A scorecard with a FAIL result does not mean the drill failed in a harmful way -- it means the drill found a gap. The correct response is to update the runbook, fix the tooling, or add a pre-requisite check, then re-run the drill to confirm the fix.
Common Gaps Found in Drills
Gap
Root Cause
Fix
Flag CLI not installed
On-call laptop does not have launchdarkly-cli
Add to standard on-call setup checklist; verify in onboarding
Prometheus query returns no data
Staging metric name differs from production
Parameterize metric names; run query validation as part of drill setup
Rollback took >7 min
Pod readiness probe timeout too long (default 300s)
Tune readiness probe failureThreshold and periodSeconds
Status update delayed
Communication template URL was not in runbook
Add template URL and pre-filled channel name to runbook Step 5
Wrong image tagged as stable
Stable image environment variable was stale
Add image tag verification step to drill procedure
Data migration rollback failed
Snapshot was not verified before migration started
Add snapshot verification to migration go/no-go checklist
Integrating Drills into the Go/No-Go Process

For a new GenAI feature to be approved for production launch, the rollback drill for that feature must have been executed and passed within the past 30 days. This is a hard gate on the go/no-go checklist: an untested rollback path is not a rollback path.
Feature flag drill: executed in staging with the exact flag key that will be used in production.
Service rollback drill: confirmed that kubectl rollout undo restores the stable image in under 7 minutes.
Data migration rollback drill: executed if any schema migration is part of the release.
Drill scorecard filed: link to the scorecard commit in the go/no-go ticket.
Gaps resolved: any FAIL items from the drill have been fixed and the drill re-run.
Summary

Rollback drills validate that the rollback runbook works as documented, the on-call engineer can execute it without outside help, and the time-to-recovery meets the SLA. The four drill types -- feature flag (monthly), full service rollback (quarterly), data migration rollback (before each migration), and game day (semi-annual) -- provide progressive confidence that all rollback paths are functional. Scorecards make drill outcomes auditable and create a historical record that drives runbook improvements. No GenAI feature should launch to production without at least one successful rollback drill executed in staging within the past 30 days.
